title: Rule model and config loader (.jit/rules.toml)
description:
Define the rule data model and the `.jit/rules.toml` loader, including selector matching and the shorthand-versus-raw-schema guard.

## Background

A rule is the declarative unit the engine evaluates: selector + assertion + severity + enforce flag. The loader parses `.jit/rules.toml` and, for any issue, computes the union of applicable rules. See `dev/active/f6a704d0-validation-engine-plan.md` ("The Rule model") and decision record sections 3, 4, 7.2, 8.1.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] [hard] `validation/rules.rs` defines `Rule { name, when: Selector, severity, enforce, assert, scope }`, with `Selector` over `type`/`label`/`state`/`has_doc_type` and `Severity { off, warn, error }`.
- [ ] [hard] `.jit/rules.toml` parses into `Vec<Rule>`; an absent `enforce` defaults to false.
- [ ] [hard] Selector matching is AND across present dimensions; a `matching_rules(issue)` returns the union of all rules whose selector matches; `label` selectors support `ns:*` wildcards.
- [ ] [hard] An `assert` table holds exactly one kind; combining a shorthand kind with `json-schema` in one rule is a config error.
- [ ] [hard] Schemas load inline (as `serde_json::Value`) or from a `.jit/schemas/<name>.json` path; TOML to serde_json round-trips faithfully (regex backslashes via TOML literal strings), covered by a unit test.
- [ ] [hard] A rule's `scope` (local vs graph) is derived from its assertion kind.

## Notes

Anchors: config loading in `config_manager.rs` / `config.rs`; `toml` 0.8 dependency. The shorthand-XOR-raw guard implements decision record 8.1.
